articles.title: Daniel Cormier REACTS to Justin Gaethje TAKING SHOTS AT Ilia Topuria before White House title fight
articles.description: Justin Gaethje called out Ilia Topuria in the media, saying he "would leave him too" if he was Ilia's ex wife. Things have gotten personal and Daniel Cormier IS BACK to give his take on the build up to the championship fight set to main event the UFC's historic White House card next week! DC argues that this is exactly what this fight needs, and why the matchups on this card including Alex Pereira vs. Ciryl Gane and Derrick Lewis vs. Josh Hokit have also gotten more momentum in the recent weeks. articles.title: Alex Pereira Doesn't Believe Tom Aspinall is Next
articles.description: Chael just spoke directly with Alex Pereira and his coach, and the conversation raised several unexpected questions. Would an interim heavyweight title make Pereira the first-ever three-division champion? Surprisingly, Pereira doesn't see it that way. He told Chael that "champ-champ-champ" is a label for the fans to decide, not something he would claim for himself. The bigger surprise may have been what Pereira had to say about Tom Aspinall. According to Alex, he doesn't expect Aspinall to be ready anytime soon, and if Pereira wins at heavyweight, he isn't counting on Tom being his next fight. That creates a fascinating question: if not Aspinall, then what's next? Chael also reveals what Pereira's team told him about Alex's new UFC deal and why they believe the promotion can put any opponent, in any weight class, in front of him. Heavyweight, light heavyweight, interim title, undisputed title—it doesn't matter. Pereira plans to stay active. Plus, Chael explores the scenario nobody is discussing: what happens if Pereira loses? 0:00 Alex Pereira reacts to "champ-champ-champ" 0:43 Does an interim belt count?1:07 Pereira's surprising answer 1:34 Why the achievement may not matter to him 2:00 Pereira doesn't expect to fight Tom Aspinall 2:33 Aspinall's injury timeline 3:10 The interim title problem 3:47 What if Pereira loses? 4:27 Pereira's coach reveals details of new UFC deal 5:07 "We'll fight anybody" 5:45 Why Chael appreciates Pereira's approach 6:21 The biggest takeaway from the conversation Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Why 170 Is NOT an Advantage for Conor #ufc
articles.description: A lot of fans believe fighting at welterweight gives Conor McGregor a competitive advantage — but Chael Sonnen completely rejects that argument. In this breakdown, Chael explains why cutting to lightweight is actually proof of discipline, preparation, and focus, not a disadvantage. Chael responds to claims that Conor is somehow more dangerous at 170 pounds because he doesn’t have to suffer through a difficult weight cut. He compares the logic to fighters moving between every division in MMA, including featherweight, lightweight, and flyweight. According to Chael, once both athletes enter the cage on fight night, they weigh roughly the same anyway — so where exactly is the “advantage”? He also addresses comparisons involving Max Holloway moving up in weight and explains why fans misunderstand what weight classes are really testing. Chael’s core argument is simple: harder preparation creates sharper fighters. Suggesting that less work somehow equals a better competitive position completely misses the point of elite combat sports. This clip dives deep into weight cutting, MMA conditioning, fight preparation, and the psychology behind how fans evaluate size advantages in the UFC. For more UFC analysis, Conor McGregor updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #ConorMcGregor #UFC #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA #Welterweight #CombatSports

articles.title: Why Fighting Outside Changes Everything #ufc
articles.description: Joe Rogan raised concerns about the rumored UFC White House event being held outdoors — but Chael Sonnen thinks that criticism may actually prove why the idea works. If mixed martial arts is supposed to simulate a real fight, then why should the conditions be perfectly controlled? In this clip, Chael reacts to Joe Rogan questioning the unpredictability of an outdoor event, including weather, atmosphere, and changing conditions. Chael explains why that uncertainty may actually bring MMA closer to the reality fighters always claim to prepare for. A true street fight doesn’t happen under arena lights with climate control and rehearsed routines. It happens in chaos. Chael also highlights comments from Ilia Topuria, who admitted the idea feels strange because it’s difficult to visualize and prepare for. To Chael, that honesty from a champion reveals exactly why the concept is fascinating. The inability to predict conditions is not a flaw — it’s the first element of a real fight. This breakdown dives into the psychology of combat sports, fighter preparation, and why the UFC’s White House concept may be more authentic than critics realize. For more MMA analysis, UFC reactions, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, stay tuned for more clips every day. #UFC #JoeRogan #IliaTopuria #ChaelSonnen #MMA #UFCTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: “You Helped Me Retire” DJ On Retiring From MMA
Ciryl Gane Thinks the Weather Matters
articles.description: Ciryl Gane says the outdoor conditions in Washington, D.C. could create a wrinkle in a potential fight with Alex Pereira. Chael isn't buying it. In this episode, Chael breaks down the long-standing debate over weather, humidity, elevation, and environmental conditions in combat sports. From Cain Velasquez's loss to Fabricio Werdum in Mexico City to outdoor fights, bugs, rain, heat, and humidity, Chael asks a simple question: if both athletes are competing under the exact same conditions, why should any of it matter? He also shares a lesson from his father about horse racing, track conditions, and the stories people tell themselves before competition. Are environmental factors a real competitive advantage, or are fighters laying the groundwork for an explanation before the fight ever happens? #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #AlexPereira #CirylGane 0:00 Ciryl Gane raises concerns about outdoor conditions 0:48 Werdum vs Cain and the Mexico City lesson 1:55 Chael's experience training in extreme heat 2:18 The horse racing lesson from his father 3:13 Why weather should affect both fighters equally 4:38 Fighters complain more than other athletes 5:25 Why fans should care more than fighters 6:02 The problem with weather-based excuses 6:32 Chael's final argument against the narrative Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Alex Pereira Was Already a Heavyweight...
articles.description: Joe Rogan raised an interesting question: will Alex Pereira slow down at heavyweight? It's a fair concern. More weight usually comes with tradeoffs. Fighters gain size, but they often lose speed, cardio, or mobility in the process. The problem is Alex Pereira may not actually be getting bigger. Chael breaks down Pereira's training camp numbers and explains why the former UFC champion has spent years training at roughly the same weight he'll carry into his heavyweight showdown with Ciryl Gane. If Pereira has already been doing his camps at heavyweight size, is there really anything new happening here? Along the way, Chael examines Rogan's theory, Rico Verhoeven's surprising assessment of the matchup, and why fans may be overlooking an outcome that nobody seems willing to discuss. Because before we assume heavyweight changes Alex Pereira, we should probably ask whether he's been a heavyweight all along. #UFC #MMA #AlexPereira #JoeRogan #CirylGane #TomAspinall #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Joe Rogan's Pereira Theory 0:18 The Tradeoff of Added Weight 0:57 Pereira's Real Number 1:24 Why This Isn't New 2:03 The Outcome Nobody Is Considering 2:35 Rico Verhoeven's Prediction 3:00 Are We Getting a Kickboxing Match? 3:25 What Happens If Pereira Loses? 3:57 The Tom Aspinall Connection 4:31 Why Chael Disagrees 4:55 The Real Speed Question Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: What Does Eddie Hearn Actually Do for Tom Aspinall?
articles.description: Eddie Hearn recently claimed he could pay Tom Aspinall three times what the UFC pays him. That sounds impressive... until you start looking at the details. Chael breaks down the strange relationship between Eddie Hearn and Tom Aspinall, questions what Hearn's actual role is, and examines whether the promise of bigger paydays really means what people think it means. If Tom already has one of the best contracts in the sport, what exactly is Eddie bringing to the table? More importantly, where would those fights even happen? From UFC contracts to boxing loopholes, Francis Ngannou, MVP, and the realities of fighter management, Chael explores a side of the business that fans rarely hear discussed openly. Because before you can understand what Eddie Hearn is offering Tom Aspinall, you first have to answer a simple question: What does Eddie Hearn actually do for Tom Aspinall? #UFC #MMA #TomAspinall #EddieHearn #DanaWhite #FrancisNgannou #ChaelSonnen 0:00 What Does Eddie Hearn Do? 0:29 How Managers Normally Get Paid 1:05 Eddie's Big Promise 1:36 Dana's Side of the Math 2:18 Where Would Tom Fight? 3:08 The Contract Myth 3:58 The Francis Ngannou Question 4:33 Is Eddie Building Something Bigger? 4:56 What About Boxing? 5:32 What Fighters Are Told 5:59 Tom Aspinall as a Pawn 6:40 The Real Question 7:11 The Math Still Doesn't Change Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Arman Tsarukyan's Message for Ilia Topuria
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan may be the most interesting athlete in MMA right now. While waiting for his UFC title opportunity, Arman continues saying yes to everything — wrestling matches, grappling events, backup fighter duties, and whatever challenge comes next. But according to Arman, none of it changes the mission. Every workout, every training session, and every competition is still about becoming UFC champion. That mindset led to an interesting message for Ilia Topuria. While the MMA world continues obsessing over a potential showdown with Islam Makhachev, Arman believes Ilia should take that fight now. Because if Topuria steps in with him first, Arman is convinced the undefeated record won't survive. Chael explains why Arman's advice actually makes sense, wh the UFC may be quietly moving toward an Arman vs. Ilia showdown, and why this fight could end up becoming one of the biggest events of the year. #UFC #MMA #ArmanTsarukyan #IliaTopuria #IslamMakhachev #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The Most Interesting Guy in MMA 0:31 Why Promoters Love Arman 1:07 The Problem With Staying Active 1:37 Arman's Surprising Training Philosophy 2:33 The Fight Nobody Is Talking About 3:20 Why Arman vs. Ilia Makes Sense 4:04 Arman's Message to Topuria 4:50 Why His Advice Is Actually Smart 5:15 The Oliveira BMF Rumor 5:54 Why Arman Deserves the Real Title Shot 6:14 Arman vs. Ilia Could Be Massive Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Jon Jones Did NOT Look Retired
articles.description: I got to witness the Jon Jones and Gable Steveson dynamic up close at Real American Freestyle, and what I saw wasn't what I expected. Like a lot of people, I assumed this was a mutually beneficial relationship between two stars. Jon gets some attention from Gable's rise, Gable gets some attention from Jon's name. That's how these things usually work. But after watching them together, talking to Jon, and seeing the way he interacted with fans all weekend, I came away with a very different impression. Jon wasn't acting like a superstar making an appearance. He looked genuinely happy to be back around wrestling. The fans wanted him there, and for maybe the first time in a long time, he seemed to be enjoying that connection. Then there was his relationship with Gable. Not a business partnership. Not a publicity stunt. A real coach-athlete relationship. It also raised a bigger question: can the UFC eventually have both Jon Jones and Gable Steveson at the top of the same division? Because if Gable becomes what many people think he can become, that conversation is coming. #UFC #JonJones #GableSteveson #MMA #RealAmericanFreestyle #ChaelSonnen 0:00 I Witnessed It Firsthand 0:27 What I Expected to See 1:25 Jon Jones and Wrestling Fans 2:51 Why This Crowd Was Different 3:19 Jon Admits He Missed It 4:00 The Side of Jon Nobody Sees 4:42 Does Jon Want to Compete Again? 5:03 Jon Doesn't Look Retired 5:39 The Real Jon-Gable Relationship 6:14 Can Both Reach The Top? 6:34 The Real Story Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Chael Debunks Wrestling’s Biggest Myth
articles.description: After watching the European Championships, Chael Sonnen was reminded of an argument he used to have constantly at the Olympic Training Center. For years, coaches and wrestling experts pointed to Russia and Europe as proof that they had discovered superior techniques, superior systems, and superior training methods. Chael never bought it. In this story, Chael revisits his legendary debates with Olympic coach Anatoly and explains why he believes the wrestling world has spent decades chasing myths instead of acknowledging the obvious. From arm drags and duck unders to gut wrenches and throws, Chael argues that the techniques haven't changed nearly as much as people claim. The real difference, according to him, comes down to athlete selection, talent pools, and a reality many people don't want to discuss. Chael also breaks down the famous Russian training systems, why American coaches became obsessed with them, and why he believes much of the mystique around Russian wrestling was built on the wrong foundation. It's one of Chael's most entertaining wrestling stories — and one he's been telling for decades. #Wrestling #FreestyleWrestling #EuropeanChampionships #ChaelSonnen #Olympics #NCAAWrestling 0:00 The European Championships0:14 Chael vs Anatoly0:50 “Coach, That’s Not True”1:21 The Techniques Haven’t Changed2:08 What Has Actually Improved2:52 Why Russia Wins Medals3:22 The Real Advantage3:40 The Russian Training Myth4:10 The Famous Periodization System4:36 Why American Coaches Bought In4:53 Chael’s Final AnswerBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Come see my show LIVE in Washington DC June 13!! Spcial guests announced soon 👀#shorts #ufc #mma
Islam vs Ilia Is the Real Fight #ufc
articles.description: Here’s the problem Islam Makhachev is dealing with right now: People don’t care about anybody else. Not because Islam isn’t great — he is. He’s the world champion for a reason. Elite skill set. Dominant style. Proven winner. But there are only two fights fans truly want: Ilia Topuria and Arman Tsarukyan. That’s it. And the criticism surrounding Islam is growing because many fans believe he avoided both matchups when the opportunities were there. Now, fair or unfair, once that narrative starts, every other fight feels smaller. If Islam goes out and beats Ian Machado Garry tomorrow, are people suddenly going to rank it above beating Ilia or Arman? No chance. Because champions get judged differently. When you’re the best in the world, fans expect you to face the most dangerous threats available — especially the ones everyone is demanding. That’s why this conversation keeps coming back. Not because Islam isn’t respected. Not because he isn’t accomplished. But because there are two names attached to his legacy now. And until those fights happen, people are going to keep asking the same question: Why not? That’s the pressure that comes with being champion. And whether the criticism is deserved or not, the expectation isn’t going away. #UFC #MMA #IslamMakhachev #IliaTopuria #ArmanTsarukyan #IanGarry #FightTalk #MMADebate #CombatSports #UFCTitlePicture

articles.title: Ronda Says She’d “Clean Holly’s Clock”
articles.description: Ronda Rousey is retired. She already said she was only coming back for one fight with Gina Carano. And yet somehow, she's still dominating the conversation. After saying she would "clean Holly Holm's clock" in a rematch, Ronda once again found herself at the center of MMA headlines. But according to Chael Sonnen, that's exactly the point. Chael breaks down why the phrase was so effective. It wasn't disrespectful. It wasn't vulgar. It wasn't even particularly aggressive. But it accomplished exactly what every great promoter hopes to accomplish: it got people talking. Chael also examines Ronda's claim that beating Holly would "rewrite UFC history," a statement he believes was carefully crafted to provoke debate without crossing the line into trash talk. Whether Ronda fully believes everything she's saying or is simply playing the game better than everyone else, Chael sees a pattern emerging. Once again, Ronda Rousey found a way to stay relevant, stay in the headlines, and keep the conversation centered around her. And that's why Chael calls it another win. #RondaRousey #HollyHolm #ChaelSonnen #UFC #MMA #GinaCarano 0:00 Ronda’s Holly Holm Comment 0:24 Why “Clean Her Clock” Works 0:52 The Most Likeable Version of Ronda Yet 1:14 Is Ronda Serious or Performing? 1:46 What She Did Right During Gina Carano Build-Up 2:17 Ronda’s Pride Won’t Let Holly Go 2:47 Why the Phrase Matters 3:11 The “Rewrite UFC History” Claim 3:42 Why That Statement Provokes Debate 4:00 You Can’t Rewrite History Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Dana White Confirmed Chimaev’s Plan
articles.description: Dana White just confirmed what many suspected: Khamzat Chimaev is actively pushing for a title shot against Sean Strickland. And according to Chael Sonnen, that matters more than most people realize. Chael explains why the real battle isn't happening in the Octagon right now — it's happening in interviews, headlines, social media, and public perception. While Chimaev is publicly campaigning for the fight, Nassourdine Imavov remains largely silent despite having a legitimate argument for being next in line. Chael breaks down how title fights are actually secured, why fan interest matters, and why Sean Strickland may have already handed Imavov a huge advantage by publicly endorsing him as the rightful next challenger. But endorsements only go so far. From media strategy to public demand, Chael argues that Chimaev and Imavov are currently competing for the same opportunity — and only one of them appears to be actively fighting for it. This isn't a fight for rankings. It's a fight for attention. #UFC #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #NassourdineImavov #ChaelSonnen #MMA #DanaWhite 0:00 Dana White Confirms Chimaev's Push 0:33 The Only Other Contender Is Imavov 0:58 Why Chael Hasn't Heard From Imavov 1:39 What Chael Did When He Wanted a Title Shot 2:12 Chimaev's Team Is Working the Media 2:49 Chimaev vs Imavov Is Already Happening 3:03 Sean Strickland's Endorsement Matters 3:24 The Forgotten Strickland-Imavov Fight 3:52 A Fight for Clicks and Attention 4:29 Why Chimaev Has the Momentum 4:48 Imavov Has to Speak Up 5:05 The Rule Nobody Can Ignore Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: What Happened to Bob Menery? #ufc
articles.description: I was genuinely caught off guard watching this. Bob Menery is a guy I’ve watched for years. Fighters go on his show constantly. Big personality, always joking around, always asking fighters the same question: “How fast could you beat me in a street fight?” And every fighter laughs it off. Even Logan Paul had to straighten him out one time. But walking to the ring this time? Bob didn’t look like himself. He looked extremely thin. Jittery. Off. The kind of energy where you instantly notice something isn’t right. I’m not accusing him of anything — not drugs, not alcohol, none of that. I’m saying visually, from my own experience being around that environment growing up, he looked wired up. Like somebody running on stimulants and adrenaline at the same time. And then he gets in there with Johnny Manziel. People forget: Manziel is a real athlete. Heisman Trophy winner. First-round NFL talent. Explosive movement, instincts, timing — that stuff doesn’t just disappear. And once the fight started? It looked less like a celebrity match and more like a street fight with an actual athlete. Ugly. Fast. Chaotic. That’s what made the whole thing so uncomfortable to watch. Because the energy coming in already felt strange — and the fight only amplified it. #BobMenery #JohnnyManziel #LoganPaul #Boxing #CelebrityBoxing #FightNews #CombatSports #MMA #SportsDrama #FightTalk

articles.title: Joe Rogan Raised a Strange Point!
articles.description: Joe Rogan has always argued that combat sports are ultimately about discovering what works in a real fight. And that's exactly why Chael Sonnen was surprised by one of Rogan's recent comments about the proposed UFC event at the White House. According to Chael, if the entire purpose of mixed martial arts is to test fighting under realistic conditions, then why would fighting outdoors be viewed as a negative? From the early days of the UFC and the rise of Royce Gracie to the evolution of wrestling, boxing, Muay Thai, and takedown defense, Chael revisits the original idea behind mixed martial arts: finding out what actually works when the rules are stripped away. He also reacts to Ilia Topuria's fascinating comments about visualization and preparation, explaining why uncertainty itself may be one of the most realistic elements fighters can face. If weather, environment, and unexpected conditions are all part of real-world conflict, maybe the White House setting is closer to the spirit of MMA than many people realize. #UFC #JoeRogan #IliaTopuria #ChaelSonnen #MMA #WhiteHouseUFC 0:00 Joe Rogan’s Longtime Philosophy 0:36 What Works in a Real Fight? 1:20 Why Chael Agrees With Joe 1:55 The Royce Gracie Problem 2:10 Joe’s White House Comment 2:43 Why Does “Outside” Matter? 3:15 Fighting in the Rain3:36 Real Fights Don’t Have Perfect Conditions 3:50 Ilia Topuria’s Fascinating Observation 4:25 Why Uncertainty Matters 4:50 Chael’s Problem With the Criticism Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Conor McGregor’s Welterweight Advantage Is a Myth
articles.description: Is fighting at welterweight a competitive advantage for Conor McGregor? Chael Sonnen says absolutely not. After hearing the growing argument that Conor benefits from fighting at 170 pounds instead of cutting to lightweight, Chael explains why the logic doesn't hold up. In his view, making weight isn't a disadvantage — it's proof of discipline, focus, conditioning, and hard work. Drawing from wrestling, the Olympics, and his own experience, Chael argues that athletes only work as hard as they have to. The harder the weight cut, the more discipline and preparation are required. Remove that challenge, and you may remove some of what made the athlete dangerous in the first place. Chael also revisits comments Conor previously made about weight cutting, discusses why fighters often perform best when they force themselves into elite shape, and shares the story of a wrestler who became nearly unbeatable only after getting down to his competition weight. The question isn't whether Conor can fight at 170. The question is why anyone thinks it helps him. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA #UFCNews 0:00 Is 170 a Competitive Advantage for Conor? 0:16 Chael Strongly Disagrees 0:45 Athletes Only Work as Hard as They Have To 1:22 Why Making Weight Matters 1:55 155 vs 170 — Which Is Harder? 2:27 Conor’s View on Weight Cutting 3:00 Why Welterweight Isn't an Advantage 3:36 Weight Classes Aren't What Fans Think 4:06 The Josh Hokit Example 4:34 Doug Lee's Secret Weapon 5:04 Why Weight Cuts Create Killers 5:18 What's the Evidence? 5:30 "You Sound a Little Stupid" Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench
